channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off. — 35 replies — 140.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off.

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-06-09 22:37 UTC

OP

Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off. I told him that was good.

I told him that most people will not make it in today’s economy when $150,000 is considered middle class. He replied that $150,000 is a lot of money. I said sure, but the average American salary is around $65,000.

Then I asked him what kind of work he did, and he said he did some low level blue collar welding. I told him blue collar work is terrible. He responded that welders can make over $100,000 a year.

I told him that blue collar work is for low-IQ, uneducated people and that the adults in those jobs are losers and people who messed up their lives. I also said blue collar work is fun when you’re a kid, but the adults doing it are there because they’re losers.

It turned out that three blue collar workers had been listening to our conversation and told the kid not to listen to me because I didn’t know what I was talking about.

I then told the kid that when he grows up, he will realize people look down on blue collar workers for a reason and that blue collar adults are not the kind of people he should want to know.

The blue collar workers called me ignorant, yet, from my perspective, they seemed to have chips on their shoulders and came across as unintelligent.

Why are blue- collar workers like this? Are they aware of how the world perceives them?

I wanted to talk numbers because no way most blue collar makes over $100k. Can someone fill me in?

Early replies (first 3)

62342722
>>62342700
Fuck off

62342735
>>62342700
I like this spam, because it counters the AI brainwashing (trades good, factory good, college bad, IT arrogant & overpaid or Indians, close the borders).

62342744
>>62342735
What?
>>62342722
Fuck you.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62342794
>>62342735

Brown hands typed this

62342798
>>62342722
It's just bot threads. It all seems pointless. They have these weird topics they spam.
I think they try to get people used to a certain topic like "my bf doesn't flush his poop" using the Metre-Exposure effect. Then they have an "influencer" read a script written by LLM.
https://www.instagram.com/reel/DTLEuB7jeXj/
If you don't have IG like me:
https://imginn.com/reel/DTLEuB7jeXj/

62342805
>>62342756
>It isn’t spam. It was a conversation I had today

62342807
>>62342798
Take your meds.

62342811
>>62342770
> There are people who spend all day working and those who don't. There are people who don't work who live off investments/passive income, and those who live off welfare.
You literally just described the class there.

62342828
>reddit spacing, the thread

62342834
>>62342700

62342837
Trades are horrible. You make decent money, but it destroys your body over time. Don’t want to deal with the female HR humiliation ritual in a role for college graduates? Go be a fucking engineer. Anyone who says trades are based or awesome is expressing the most gargantuan amount of cope.

62342838
>>62342807
sarr? bad you mean, sarr?

62342844
>>62342700

another

62342849
>>62342756

No, it wasn't. Everyone knows you're lying. Your izzat is destroyed

62342859
>>62342700
>teh thread

62342866
>>62342828
>sucking this anon's cock until he cums: me

62342869
>>62342700
Any good tradie hate memes? Or the video of the dude with cockroaches all over him

62342888
>>62342828
It is to help you plebs read.

62342892
Stop making this thread you fucking loser

62342903
>>62342892
I actually talk about /biz/ to people and sharing the conversation here for /biz/ to discuss.
Discuss or fuck off.

62342905
>>62342859
He drives a BMW.
You drive a ford.

62342934
>>62342700

kys opcd

62342939
>>62342700
>>62342744
>>62342756
>>62342807
>>62342811
>>62342888
>>62342903
>>62342905
Any life is better then to keep making the same threads on /biz/ and spamposting in it

62342948
>>62342700
City tards will be dead within 10 years from starvation and disease. Laugh it up while you can.

62342997
>>62342948
Fuck you ruralfag

62343037
>>62342700
kill yourself you stupid fucking r*ddit spacing bot.

62343068
all you fags are fags.

Blue collar work is important also. Sure wiring can be done pre-fab for new construction, but here's a fun fact: 100% of all the houses that have already been built have already been built.
That means that their cast iron pipes are about to collapse, or their pvc pipes are about to come unglued or crack. It means that if they want to redo the kitchen, someone has to come tear out the old cabinets and install new ones and install new light fixtures and move the gas line and put a heated tile floor in.

It means that the HVAC company you hire to do that has equipment that breaks, and when you shove baby wipes in the toilet and it clog, you have to hire gus to come snake that shit out.

I have a friend who's son is slightly autist and apprenticed as a welder at age 15. At 16, he got hired by a company to be the shop welder for the summer: $35/hr, 40 hrs a week. That's $70k a year, but remember, he was only 16.

Anyway, you can live a good life on $100k if:
You buy a small house and do your own repairs instead of having to hire some other dildo to do it.
Get a 6 year old Tacoma and drive it for 20 years

Once you have those costs under control, you should have lots of spare money to invest.

62343110
>>62342700
>child labor

62343166
>>62343110
Not really. Better than him not doing shit all summer. He already has to take 5 days off.

62343167
>>62342798
>da booooooots
Kek

62343175
>>62343166
child labor is worse than not doing shit all summer. At that age he should study, fuck and do drugs instead of adopting the goy mentality

62343255
>>62343175
There's absolutely zero things wrong with child labor when it involves doing normal ass jobs adults do with the same standards.

People always jump to kids being kept as slaves or beaten or made to carry explosives or work in some dangerous factory without training and it's stupid.

There's zero reason why a 12 year old kid shouldn't be alloweded to get paid to wipe down tables at a McDonald's or bus tables at Applebee's over his summer.

62343264
>>62342700
Had a "white collar" semi-richfag come to me one time for help. He was scared to death to open the radiator cap so he could add water and I told him how to check by pressing the upper hose to see whether there was any pressure or not. Come to find out he didn't even know where to add more water, he didn't know what the radiator cap was or how to open it but was adamant about how dangerous it was so he wouldn't do it. How do these people drive a car?

item.title: ICP — 41 replies — 67.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
ICP

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-06-09 22:08 UTC

OP

>we have steve jobs at home

Early replies (first 3)

62336952
>>62336934
soon

62337106
>>62336934
ICP has the best tech

62337150
>>62337106
ICUP

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62337372
>>62337356
UN

62337376
>>62337316
I am ICP Chad but let’s not lie. Amazon went down ~90% after Dotcom crash and only once. ICP has gone down ~90% many times over (scam pump followed by crash) and is now down ~99% from ath. ICP is being repressed 10x more than Amazon because it is 10x a bigger deal

62337385
>>62337376
That’s because you’re ill informed. $700 was never the ATH, it was more like $80.
Hunchbacks used futures to manipulate the price at launch. That $700 literally lasted for a few hours before they deliberately crashed it.

62337420
>>62337376
Forget back -90%, nobody profited from this since Moonman shilled this around $50, the last semi good pump was from $3 to $20, that was the last pump, the somehow tech couldn't keep any pump because the rat of switzerland just keeps extracting, OGs were raped, early adopters never got anything back

62337568
>>62337420
Your obsession with moonman is actually a mental illness.
ICP is now far more advanced and mature than it was in 2024 when we reached almost $20.
The next pump will be much more extreme because ICP has had many adoptions this year.
You need to stop fudding, it’s really not healthy for you.

62337596
>>62337420
Also, mission 70 already cut inflation by 40%. The tokenomics are much better now than when ICP was worth $50. We just need an alt season to get this party going.

62337713
>>62337372
>icy pee is partnered with the foremost advocate of white genocide
bearish

62337760
>>62336934
I am considering dumping 10k into this. It might fail spectacularly but it’s the lowest MC and highest potential coin I can think of. I am tired of working myself to death.

62337787
>>62336934
Dis-lui que je lui passe le bonjour

62337830
>>62337760
That’s a good mindset. Worst case scenario you lose a few thousand dollars or you make half a million when the AI alt season starts.

62337832
>>62337385
I understand the futures Sam Barkman scam. I was going off a $200 top. Even $80 top puts ICP down 97% however

62337836
>>62337760
10k is a good gamble for something that has potential to retire you, just don’t over extend because the risk is big. ICP is imo still the most exciting narrative besides chainlink in crypto right now

62337916
>>62337836
Chainlink is not that exciting lol

62337929
>>62336934
Lol the black turtleneck lives on

62337937
>>62337760
For your own good, don't, trust me, i've been suckered into this scam for 6 years, it has fundamental issues stemming from it's core team, they are ok with the price hovering low just surviving and extracting

62337958
>>62337787
are you talking about mm?

62338026
>>62337937
I’ve held on and off for years as well. But seems like things are starting to click now.

62338066
>>62338026
Don’t listen to him, he’s a known fudder on biz, he hated ICP for years, his obsession with moonman is a mental illness.

62338597
>>62337916
Chainlink is very exciting, they research so much

62338901
>>62336934

62339051
>>62336934
>his first name on his handle is in lower caps
Woah these zoomers are so young and hip. I bet he's like 21 or something.

62339665
please fuck my ass

62339717
>>62337372
Looks like the entire top 30 is in that same list. What the fuck are you doing.

62340134
>>62339717
Dfinity is the most important because they have a partnership with the government of Pakistan a country with 100 million people.

62341606
>>62336934
.

62342451
>>62338066
will we moon this year?

62343082
>>62342451
Yes brother. Jerry is the OG

62343093
>>62337365
>1. ICP was launched 5 years ago not 5

62343103
>>62337568
>ICP has had many adoptions this year.
You are Indian

62343169
>>62342451
what holding ICP does to a mf

item.title: How do you actually cash out? (a large amount) — 45 replies — 54.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
How do you actually cash out? (a large amount)

Open thread • Replies: 45 • Updated: 2026-06-09 21:55 UTC

OP

I'm planning on selling a decent chunk of my stack next year in order to pay for house construction.

Has anyone sold a large amount OTC and had the funds deposited in a US or European bank account? Any tips?

I'm talking 6 to 7 figures

Early replies (first 3)

62336656
>>62336622
cash out?

62336664
>>62336622
You talk to a tax consultant and let your bank know in advance that you have big crypto deposits coming in and to not flag your account

62336685
>>62336664
thanks, but how do you convert the crypto in the first place? OTC company, crypto exchange, something else?

do you know if some banks are more tolerant than others? i have accounts in the US and Europe

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62336920
Oh yes, absolutely. Because nothing says "carefully planned home construction" quite like tying your entire building budget to one of the most volatile asset classes on Earth and then assuming every intermediary involved will roll out a red carpet while a mountain of money teleports into your bank account.
In this magnificent circus act, the market will obviously cooperate, your chosen exit date will coincide with peak prices, the OTC desk will operate with the speed of a Formula 1 pit crew, your bank's compliance department will greet the incoming wire with confetti cannons, and tax authorities will simply nod approvingly from a distance. What could possibly go wrong?
The fantasy gets even better. You're building a house, a project famous for never experiencing delays, cost overruns, contractor disputes, permit issues, material shortages, or surprise expenses. Pair that with an asset that can decide to impersonate a falling piano at any moment, and you've created the financial equivalent of juggling chainsaws while riding a unicycle across a tightrope made of spaghetti.
Then comes the banking adventure. People talk about large transfers as if they're ordering a pizza. In reality, moving significant sums often involves documentation, source-of-funds checks, compliance reviews, questions, more questions, and occasionally questions about the questions. Yet somehow the expectation is that a giant OTC sale will produce a magical wire transfer that glides through the global financial system like a bald eagle soaring through a sunset.
The best part is the assumption that next year will conveniently be the perfect time to sell. Not six months earlier. Not six months later. Exactly when the concrete needs pouring and the invoices arrive. That's not financial planning; that's consulting a crystal ball while wearing clown shoes.
By all means, have an exit strategy.

62337140
>>62336656
this reminded me of 2017 when crypto was being talked about in the mainstream now and people started asking about whether or not you could actually cash out of crypto lol

62337148
>>62336873
God bless your soul

62337230
>>62336892
the issue will be with the bank that will freeze the account and question you like a criminal. should be resolvable but still a pain in the ass and lets you know who really is the one in control

62337875
>>62336622
the Jews will never allow it

62337902
>>62336920
>an asset that can decide to impersonate a falling piano at any moment
made me chuckle

62337909
>>62336920
funny clanker

62337949
>>62336622
I had huge issues cashing out. Tried to sell on the same exchange where I bought years ago and they wouldn't let me. Beside all the kyc bullshit, had to prove the source of the initial funds, even though they let me buy with no questions asked back then. A nightmare.

62338205
>>62336763
Took out 100k in the summer via Kraken to Revolut. No questions asked at all

62338346
>>62338205
part of making it is triggering the automated unusual activity detections that lead to interrogations or account suspensions on those less caring about individual customers

62338356
>>62336664
I didn't even tell the bank. Just transferred $300K straight from coinbase. They didn't even call like they have for some other large deposits. I did let the accountant know upfront but only because I had to talk with him about some other stuff. If your bank gives you shit about having money then you should get another bank.

62338965
>>62336622
I cashed out mid 6 figure via coinbase, transfer to my regular local bank. It was literally nothing, I did go see bank branch manager and told the the deposit was Coming and wanted to no fuckery, he understood but also said it was literally no issue.
After I did this, they started and have continued to give me all my deposits from job immediately, they don't sit on them for days like they do to all the plebs.
I hate banks so much.

62338979
I cashed about 80k from coinbase, not the same as your numbers but zero issues here. Didn't tell anyone I was doing it, no one cared, I figured I am a small fry.

62338985
>>62336622
You can’t cash out. Every major CEX will freeze your account immediately when you try and you’ll lose your money. They’ve done it for less. The only way is use multiple Asian banks at 20k a piece. Don’t even risk sending more than 10k to goybase a year

62338990
>>62338985
Why do I always see these posts? Who makes them? Simply not true and I don't believe you've experienced this.

62339110
>>62338990
It's true tho. The bank just denies the transaction and it's unable to be recovered.

62339183
>>62336622
depends on the liquidity. if it's some shitcoin with crappy liquidity you either want to slowly swap or otc. if it's eth or something you should not even be worried about cashing out 6 to 7 figures. just find the exchange with the lowest fees, dex or cex. if you can swap it to usdc on a dex for less fees than selling on a cex you can then send it over to coinbase to cash out with no fee.

62339195
>>62339110
This. Do it piece by piece.

The banks know crypto will kill them, and they've paid regulators to go along with it.

They can just block the transaction, and the exchange won't give a fuck and say it's gone.

62339234
>>62336622
when you want to cash out, you switch to DAI or USDT on TRX. then you find someone you trust locally that wants to give you $ in person. that's it.

62339239
>>62336920
>that's not x, it's x
ai posters should be maimed and tortured

62339497
UHH GUYS I CAN'T CASH OUT

62339725
>>62339497
Don't worry. You are the kind of trader who doesnt need to cash out

62339749
>>62339110
>>62339195
>your money will just like dissapear
Again, 1pbtid making up shit..

62339794
>>62336622
>Any tips?
Trocador and Crypto ATMs.

62340092
>>62336622
Get a Uphold debit card and spend it through that. You get 2% back in crypto. If you pay $100 you will get 4% back in crypto instead which is worth it at that level of purchase. Or buy gold with it and collateralize the loan against it.

62340190
>>62336685
Obviously you need to sell for cash first. Don’t just place a $800k sell order unless it’s Bitcoin on Coinbase and even then I’d do 8 orders of $100k separately. Use the biggest exchange you can. You don’t need OTC for sub 7 figures.

62341119
>>62337230
Banks will question you like a criminal no matter what. They're not good faith actors in any arena
>t. Had to get interrogated over 4 figures of a xmas gift from my grandmother a few years ago

62341409
>>62338965
He didnt ask for you to prove where it came from?

62341527
>>62341119
Ever heard about AML/KYC compliance? It’s not like the banks don’t enjoy taking your money. Governments strong-armed banks into playing bad cop and snitch with their own customers.

This way, governments are effectively outsourcing law enforcement to banks, as well as lawyers and accountants, while *getting paid* from these professionals.

The problem is the hubris of big government, as always.

62343132
You guys have gains to cash out??

item.title: Canadian job market — 20 replies — 19.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Canadian job market

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-06-09 21:42 UTC

OP

it's so cooked holy shit. I've been applying to jobs for 2 months now and no one is returning. I've been spending hours making custom resumes and cover letters. I'm overqualified in most cases too. NO ONE HAS RETURNED.

Early replies (first 3)

62341141
>>62341035
>I've been applying to jobs for 2 months now and no one is returning
7 months here, 3 interviews, time to start goofmaxxing

62341445
>>62341035
Day of the rake has arrived

62341463
your domain?
job title you are aiming for?
years of experience?
salary expectation?
education?
certifications?

im hr in quebec

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62341598
>>62341035
All low level work prefers jeets because gov subsidizes their wage and they only have to pay them half, all medium to high end work requires nepotism, if no connections then they value DEI, only jobs available to white people are local smaller companies that don’t have to play by dei rules. Unfortunately these companies tend to be very toxic and upwards mobility is impossible unless you can make friends with the manager or boss, all that matters is charisma. You work hard and do your job well? No promotions for you, they will keep you where you are because why move you from a position where you make the owners a lot of money

62341640
>>62341035
Took me 6 months straight out of uni with an engineering BSc degree in 2023. That was pre-AI in Europe. Good luck.

62341662
>>62341035
Canada hasn't had a real job market since 2008. The market for helping people find jobs is bigger than the actual job market. There was a small window of opportunity right after covid when a bunch of boomers retired and companies had to hire due to severe staffing shortages, but after that it's been nothing but only jeets getting jobs.

This entire economy is captured, no one here does anything of value or makes an attempt to improve anything. The last time anyone invested in technology at all was in the 1960-80s. I work for the government and we still buy newly manufactured goods from the 80s because some boomer business got into the approved goy list back then and pulled the ladder up behind them.

If you are young and have the means, it is in your best interests to leave this god forsaken land as soon as possible or it will be the end of your bloodline.

62341882
>>62341463
Was entrepreneur in automotive & aerospace software for most of my life, now retired, I still cannot figure out why exactly the corpo candidate reqs are a mixture of high school popularity contest and Indian tier stamp bureaucracy. Always let my teams hire, half an hour interview max for basic concepts, short trial contract, if candidate worked, permanent contract. We never had corporate masks or other normalized mental illness. I recently saw a few new certs for workers and orgs, management and processes, what an absolute clown show. If AI doesn't do end to end dev soon, expect crashes.

62341896
Saar surely you have at least 50 relatives from your home country living in Canada who can give you a job saar

62341994
Bite the bullet, learn a trade and earn 80-100k/y.
At least you'll not have to face this shitty job market

62342029
>>62341994
By the time he did that, the AI goggles for unskilled workers are coming, and robots shortly after.

62342040
>>62341035
Indians who have fabricated over qualification are being hired for fractions what they would hire you for. Same thing is happening in America.

62342089
>>62341035
>custom cover letters
Lmao. The humiliation rituals you dumb goyim put yourself through to get noticed. You really think some company is going to read your gay ass little love letter begging them to hire you?

62342277
>>62341662
big true

62342288
>>62341035
Walk in the door and ask to talk to someone.
I only hire people that actually come in the building.

62342294
>>62342288
good for you, but no one wants to work at your gay male strip club

62342304
>>62342294
>never walked in and tried
>tells himself no point
>is cuck
>will never

62342322
>>62341994
My biggest mistake. I knew Aİ was gonna fuck everything over.

>>62342089
Kek cover letter are the least of it. I've also recorded videos of myself.

62342344
>>62341662
>If you are young and have the means, it is in your best interests to leave this god forsaken land as soon as possible or it will be the end of your bloodline.
And go where? Moving will just get you a bigger salary but if you can't get your foot in the door you're not going to make it anywhere.

>>62341994
>Bite the bullet, learn a trade and earn 80-100k/y.
trades are full of nepotism

62343073
>>62342322
It's not too late. It took 1.5 years to learn mine, I graduated at 29yo. Found a decent paying job right away, I'll never have to deal with HR bullshit and endless chasing of fake job offer

62343084
>>62342029
Simply not happening. Most of trade jobs outside of factory ones are extremely hard to automate and we have to constantly deal with unexpected bullshit like rust, seized shit, stripped thread and so on

item.title: No regrets selling BTC?? — 20 replies — 12.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
No regrets selling BTC??

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-06-09 21:11 UTC

OP

Some talk about regretting it.

Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move?

If you had the cash from that sale sitting in your account today, would you buy BTC again? Because I definitely would.

Early replies (first 3)

62339349
I sold my last bitcoin last year. Shitcoin is dead. AI is dead. Time to start acting like an african american.

62339372
>>62339349
Maybe regrets for buying? Should have bought chainlink

62339382
I rotated into XMR at like $105k, no regrets at all.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62339395
>>62339382
Why monero?

62339401
>>62339328
>Anyone buying ICP?

62339417
>>62339395
why monero? people actually use it, the fact that bitcoin is transparent is actually quite a con in itself. stablecoins, ETH and its L2s (think polygon, defi, smart contracts etc) and privacy coins have genuine utility; something like HYPE has utility.

Anything with bitcoin it's just about its "heckin price" that's all it is, nobody uses it.

62339452
>>62339417
>Nobody uses Bitcoin
Bro… The largest world conflict at this very moment involves Bitcoin.

62339462
>>62339452
>2 months ago

62339510
>>62339328
I don't feel bad, I sold near 100k, 120k and 100k again to buy this dip in stocks and especially gold.
I will be rebuying BTC lower starting August.

62339716
>>62339349
Thanks for your sacrifice. The bull market appreciates your contribution.

62339730
>>62339328

No regrets here. I did have over 2BTC back in 2013-2014 and if i had just decided to hold, it would've all been gone now because the exchange they were on (BTC-E) got shut down by the DOJ and unlike with Mt.Gox, users pretty much got shafted.

You can't just pick and choose ideal past moments from life to do differently, that's not how shit works. If you had that kind of clairvoyance you could easily just win the lottery or just time the current market perfectly as if you're insider-trading to become rich within a matter of weeks.

62339878
>>62339372
Why did you regret buying?

62339883
>>62339328
Sold it with no regrets, tripled my initial investment, moved it into stocks which actually move up
Some posters laugh at boomer stocks but btc and most crypto has been going down for well over a year, even shit boomer stocks that barely beat inflation are better, at this point crypto is dead and the days of it doing massive 10x in a day will never happen again

62340066
>>62339328
>Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move?
sold 0.3 above 90k, still have 1.7 left.
i hve 31k to buy back in october.

i also sold 0.6 below 40k and i do regret that.

62340438
>>62339382
What's your price goal for xmr?

62341003
>>62339395
privacy maybe

62341032
>>62339417
you've been conned, the fact that you think eth have any utility is a clear sign of that

62341036
>>62339328
Sold at the top. No regrets. will buy back in after saylor dumps his load

62342205
>>62339401
And why would you buy this?

62342982
>>62339417
I don't think it's fair to say nobody uses Bitcoin. It's just that Bitcoin's dominant use case ended up being different from what many people originally expected.

item.title: “The keys was stolen” it was not our fault…. — 12 replies — 10.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
“The keys was stolen” it was not our fault….

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-09 21:36 UTC

OP

It is obvious this is just a lie for their insider rugpull. It’s crazy how in crypto companies can just lie and say some bullshit like they were hacked when it’s always obvious it was an insider job to extract cash out and dump on retail to absolve themselves from any criminality or lessen the fud of an actual company sell off.

Vesting, etc, nothing actually matters because of this ability for companies to be able to make up any bullshit they want for an exit scam. So when you buy something in crypto how do you actually know that you are safe from it?

EdgeX crashed 50% in a day and they simply blamed market manipulation
Today Shariah AI dumped over 50%
Humanity Protocol dumped like 80% in a day
ZEC an exploit was found casing a crash

These crazy crashes are really upping in frequency. Is this a sign of something???

Early replies (first 3)

62341038
plausible deniability
you always need to treat these projects the same as solana memes

62341048
>>62340963
Literally who? Nobody? Ok.

62341063
>>62340963
>These crazy crashes are really upping in frequency. Is this a sign of something???
These are all cz exit liq hunting, where were u in the past 9 months?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62341072
>>62341063
I literally told you 500 tines in the past two weeks, go buy spacex ipo! Donut buy eth and btc under 1500/55k!

62341265
>I want p2p libertarian internet money where everyone can just do whatever the fuck they want and no central authority can control it!
>w-w-wait no not like that!

62341289
>>62340963
If you invested in a fucking shitcoin named "Humanity" (literally a name that screams ABORT SCAM SCAM RED ALERT), you deserve everything that happened to you desu.

62341331
>>62341289
In the same vein, as soon as I saw Bankman-Fried talking about his "effective altruism" and how he was such a humble guy driving a shitty Toyota (in the Bahamas) after making his fortune in "arbitrage", I knew it was time to invest with Kraken instead. One month later FTX was bust.

62341442
>>62341331
I knew Sam Barkman was not to be trusted from the first time I heard about him because he is an ugly goblin kike. But yeah, virtue signalling is also a massive red flag.

62341569
>>62340963
>So when you buy something in crypto how do you actually know that you are safe from it
in the past people would verify the smart contract and would, at the very least, avoid buying things where the mint function was still accessible in any way. but nowadays crypto devs are retarded and lazy and put everything under at best a multisig, at worst a single key that then gets mysteriously """exploited""". It doesn't help that centralized bridges and centralized stablecoins are entrenched as the norm, which is probably why all these lazy ass former web2 devs don't give a fuck and run incredibly insecure setups, because why bother pretending it's all decentralized when 99% of liquidity depends on centralized entities claiming they have the funds backing their shit

62341676
>>62341442
Same
And also his involvement with solana is why I never touch that shit either

62341700
>>62341569
This, i blame sol, l2s and the retarded eth wannabees.

62343061
>>62340963
what kind of shitcoin names itself "humanity protocol"? lmao... what the fuck is this dogshit.

item.title: I honestly don't get — 8 replies — 7.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
I honestly don't get

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-09 21:41 UTC

OP

how anyone defends CLL at this point. I say this as someone holding since 2017 who has accumulated over 60k stinks and largely still believes in the long term viability of the investment.

But CLL seems to be completely tone deaf with what people actually want to be hearing about. I get there might be NDAs or whatever agreements that they can't disclose details about certain relationships, but completely neglecting to talk about things like staking, go into details about the fee switches, etc. -- all shit that they have full control and say over -- despite everyone asking about it nonstop for fucking years, is just absurd. Even if they were scared to talk about anything due to legal reasons they could literally just say "Hey we hear you but we're not commenting on X, Y, or Z due to legal reasons at this time." but they don't.

The guys who white knight nonstop for them are not helping anything. Personally I do not post on X and so I am not vocal in either way but I really legitimately just don't understand who is DEFENDING them at this point. Can anyone explain it?

Early replies (first 3)

62340620
tl;dr - not sellng

62340627
>>62340616
Oh also the sweeping under the rug of the catastrophe that is BUILD. Like, just fucking acknowledge it as a failure. It's mostly not even their fault. 95%+ of those projects died gruesome deaths and the tokens are worthless.

Are you guys aware CLL took custody of the tokens for most of them already (and likely sold though I have no proof of that) but just never distributed? And now there's no point in distributing because the projects are mostly legitimately dead and there's 0 liquidity for the tokens.

Just fucking say "hey, our intentions were good but things went sideways and here's why and what we plan on doing about it", but they literally do not even acknowledge what an absolute shit show BUILD became.

62341186
no honest linkies are left on this board huh, just the deranged fuddie holders and a spare few dr;ns guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62341201
>>62340616
Nice blogpost

62341204
>>62341186
nigga you bought a 2017 premined shitcoin, what did you really expect?

62341249
>>62340616
Hey, respect other people's fetishes.

There's nothing wrong with getting turned on by donating money to a fat russian scammer.

62342988
Whenever they say CLL I immediately know its a fuddie. Funny that.

62343080
KAAAAABOOOOOOOOM XRP!!

item.title: What is your plan? My plan is to DCA SOLANA in November right when it goes under $30 then hold it until 2029, I expectin… — 5 replies — 7.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
What is your plan? My plan is to DCA SOLANA in November right when it goes under $30 then hold it until 2029, I expectin…

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-09 22:02 UTC

OP

What is your plan?

My plan is to DCA SOLANA in November right when it goes under $30 then hold it until 2029, I expecting hopefully for 10x in 3 years.

Early replies (first 3)

62340162
I was hoping to at least 20x so I could retire but I am afraid of been to greed.

62340174
maybe i get some by EOY for around 20$

62340264
>>62340153
Not saying it’s a dumb plan, but it has to go as low as $30 to get "only" a 10x return after 3 years, while there are AI stocks that can do better and faster.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62340265
Converting some of my XMR to SOL when it's below 20 for an easy x2

62343157
>>62340153
Solid plan. Sol and Hype are the strongest contenders of the top 10, this cycle.
Sol might flip ETH one day.

item.title: Is anybody buying the SpaceX presale? — 6 replies — 6.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is anybody buying the SpaceX presale?

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-09 21:51 UTC

OP

I'm in the UK and can buy shares at the $135 listing. Is anybody else going to buy this?

Early replies (first 3)

62343019
chances are you preorder and will get nothing cuz everyone else is also trying to get them

62343024
>>62343001
You bet your ass I am. I am putting everything I can into this. I have sold all my other stocks and I'm all in baby. SpaceX is the most important company to go public ever. It is literally the future nobody else is taking us to space they have a complete monopoly.

62343028
>>62343024
This, elon musk is growing weed in space!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62343030
I have a feeling I can ride it Friday but I'm too scared.

62343071
ITT: the exit liquidity retail

62343120
I might put $100 buxx. Either it does a 100x or it implodes the stock market.

item.title: At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. — 185 replies — 58.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low.

Open thread • Replies: 185 • Updated: 2026-06-09 19:33 UTC

OP

At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. Logically thinking there would be at least SOME buying pressure from other people in the know and price would be held up at key points with traces of bullish absorption in the chart yet theres none of that just a fast move up into a slow bleed. If you do any type of chart watching you would know exactly what im talking about. With just a glance at the LINK chart you can tell there is not a single good/bullish thing about it.

You can't convince me people involved in these announcements with chainlink wouldn't buy some themselves and talk to their other high-class or wealthy friends and those people would buy and word would continue to spread. It just doesnt make sense unless.... the people with inside knowledge know LINK isnt necessary for the services chainlink provides.

Call me a faggot call me indian call me fudder call me retard but it just doesnt make ANY sense despite all of these announcements and the apparent fact that these giant companies are using chainlink that the LINK does not trend upward.

I dont know why im posting this I guess I just wanted to get some thoughts from you guys or rant into the void. Ive been in since 2021 and bought around 15 dollars. I'm not sure if I can hold much longer and on the other hand I dont know what I would even buy even if I sold. I wish I could invest in the company Chainlink.

Early replies (first 3)

62312631
How big is your stack

62312653
>LINK starts to moon in sats
>fuddies get riled up
Like clockwork

62312685
>>62312653
ironically this is exactly how they get away with their accumulation. either during btc dips or OTC buys. meanwhile retail looks at the price, demoralization continues, they continue to accumulate.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62335458
>>62335182
Thanks for bumping /CFG/ (Chainlink Fuddie General)

62336610
>>62332425
>You changed IDs for this? You fucking goofball.
Still here. Feel free to also refute my point on your BTC comparison falling apart due to the flow of change of hands from new BTC to node operators to market versus new Link tokens going from CLL's uncirculated supply directly to Binance instead of node operators.

62336627
>>62335458
p+c bitch

62336936
>>62316605
>Chainlink pays the nodes from their own team wallets

>>62336610
>Link tokens don't go from CLL's uncirculated supply to node operators

You're a fucking retard you know that.

62337435
>>62333330
Epstein should have stored his blackmail material in here amirite

62337475
If chainlink is decentralized why do not even the biggest, stinkiest linkies here brag about running a node?

62338077
>>62337475
linkpool made us think we could back in the day, it was all a scam, we all had a running node setup and we were waiting to be whitelisted, what a fking joke

62340215
>>62336936
You're misrepresenting my point to argue something else. Why are you doing this? You get nothing out of it unless you're a paid shill or just bored.

62340223
>>62337475
We tried. It's a full time job requiring a bunch of resources on top of the profits being nebulous thus souring a lot of people out of running their node [on AWS]. This was back in 2019 so I don't know how robust and improved running nodes is nowadays.

62340231
>>62340215
I argued against your point that "Chainlink paying the nodes from their own team wallets" decouples the link price from the usage; and then you literally asked me to refute your point that Chainlink doesn't pay the nodes from the uncirculating supply, and is instead sending the Link directly to Binance.

What the fuck are you even doing here?

62340287
>>62340231
> BTC
> No new BTC enters the market except through miners' hands.
> This means they decide and influence the price of BTC.
> This couples the BTC price to the usage of the network because miners will not sell less than the costs of operations

> Link
> new Link enters the market through payments to node operators, payments to developers, and to deals with companies
> Link node operators are no longer the sole arbiters who decide when new Link enters the market
> To access services from the network, you don't need to pay in Link, and your payments made aren't immediately converted to Link, if at all. This decouples the link token from accurately reflecting network activity
> Furthermore CLL has routine token unlocks and will also move tokens to a centralized exchange to capitalize on market movements, Ex: China's response to the Trump tariffs

https://etherscan.io/tx/0x6e347aa07b74b2330b8f3f5a9da44305cba0bf7e40b416bcf00ba9922c365fdd
https://etherscan.io/tx/0x3355d51eafaead23e063aba8a099a712a11ecb26e937f53d0850cd4c5b33801e
https://etherscan.io/tx/0x2e3b0111c94d9d0102e5822ccd9dc5f395c5b207b39a466432c1d634c4354eb6

^Above transactions took place just an hour before the great crash of October


Your job is to refute my conclusion that the link token price is NOT tied to network usage. Refute these points. You are free to disregard the BTC comparison since those are points in my favor unless you can refute my points. Any thing other than directly addressing and showcasing flaws in my logic (and I better hear something better than the low hanging fruit of "Node operators have to sell Link for cost operations too!") and I will respond with "token not needed".

62340304
>>62340287
You specifically made the point that the Link that does go through the node operators decouples the link price from the usage.
This is what I refuted.
I pointed this out to you many times but you keep pretending not to understand or something.

62340325
>>62340304
>You specifically made the point that the Link that does go through the node operators decouples the link price from the usage.

token not sneeded

62340347
>>62340325
>>62340287
>CLL has routine token unlocks and will also move tokens to a centralized exchange to capitalize on market movements
This is horseshit by the way.
He has always sold on Fridays, pretty much around the same hour, and always within 3 to 4 month intervals.

Sometimes Link pumps right after (many times in fact), sometimes some shit happens right after. But his timing is fully transparent.

62340403
>>62340347
Anon, the etherscan links posted here >>62340287 are the transactions Sergey made on the tip of what Binance was going to do in response to Trump's tariffs. For the uninitiated, Trump proposed a 100% tariff on China and China responded with crashing the market. You can dig into the archives to verify. Type in either "Trump", "China" "Binance", "Link" or whatever and date it to October.

62340410
Fuck you chainlinkies!

Sincerely,

The whole XRP community

62340414
>>62340403
>the etherscan links posted here >>62340287 are the transactions Sergey made on the tip of what Binance was going to do
Those links are the transactions Sergey made on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval.
Which has been his unlocking regimen since forever.

62340536
>>62340287
If (almost) no one uses the network, will the token price change or remain (almost) unchanged?

62340953
>>62340347
>But his timing is fully transparent.


classic boyscout bold faced lie

there is nothing transparent about the team's token sales other than them doing it 4 times per year. often it will be completely different months as years past.

didnt they sell right before 10/10/25 lol

62340971
>>62340953
>there is nothing transparent about the team's token sales
- always around 7% of total supply annually
- always on Fridays
- always within 3 to 4 month intervals

62342184
>>62340953
>didnt they sell right before 10/10/25 lol
Those are the transactions I posted that >>62340971 is trying to brush off as "part of the plan"

He's moving the goal post even when this discussion just bolsters my point about the link token price not accurately reflecting network usage. I initially wasn't thinking of the "routine token unlocks" so I'll give you my thanks.

62342243
>>62342184
You're claiming he unlocked right before the 10/10 crash to "capitalize on market movements".

The reality is he unlocked on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval. Like he's done for years.

62342296
>>62342243
Oh no no no, he most certainly did unlock just before the crash. It was part of the panic and it most certainly wasn't "luck" when even you admit it's a 3-4 month basis.

62342402
>>62342296
>he most certainly did unlock just before the crash
Who said he didn't?

>it most certainly wasn't "luck" when even you admit it's a 3-4 month basis
It was luck BECAUSE he'd already been selling on a 3-4 month basis for years at that point.

62342424
Reminder you must only say positive things, otherwise you are a shill.

62342440
>>62342424
say something nice about chainlink

62342501
>>62313866
>>62313871
Glad to see some of us are still here... I didn't do the presale but t0 Binance buyer.
It's absolutely grim the state of this project.

62342517
>>62342243
>The reality is he unlocked on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval.

yeah they do it once a quarter
this particular one we're talking about just happened to be directly before one of the largest selloffs in industry history

>but duuuuuuuuude he always does it like that

no, in years past that quarter's unlock happened in completely different months. so no it is not just like previous years.

62342559
>>62342517
>no, in years past that quarter's unlock happened in completely different months
That's because he's unlocking every 3 to 4 months. Not in the same month every time.
He even unlocks almost at the exact same time of day.

62342602
>dumps straight on the most shady CEX are "unlocks"
peak doublespeak

item.title: 65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC — 101 replies — 20.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC

Open thread • Replies: 101 • Updated: 2026-06-09 17:49 UTC

OP

>disregard dwarves
>disregard doomerfags
>if you bought the rally, hold
>if you didn't, enjoy the discount

Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value. You can buy groceries with BTC, you can't buy it with rock collections or shitcoins. And ever less the fiat bullshit can buy groceries.

>but BTC is dumping to zero--

Zoom out.

Bitcoin is the future of currency and if you haven't realized yet, you are either a fool, a scammer, or a paid shill.

Buy the fucking dip and stop selling the bottom.

‐ Yours truly, the BUYROG, pmetalsbane.

Early replies (first 3)

62309508
>>62309503
proof??

62309515
This thread now is the 64k waiting room

62309535
>>62309508
I don't need proof. I made money for everyone who bought the previous dip, who were considering cashing out and start a rock collection. And this time it isn't going to be any fucking different.

ETF returns are pathetic. PM is not investment. Bonds are tanking.

Buy BTC.

Let's get rich together.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62326550
>>62311487
ladies and gentlemen

the future of currency

62327899
>>62326531
this guy fucks

62328894
You are missing the most obvious bottom in history. And you are proud of it.

Grim.

62328962
>>62309503
are you dumb or something mate what the fuck the thing went to 58k already

62329036
>>62328962

THIS

op confirmed tard

62329055
>>62309503
intrinsic value doesn't exist
bitcoin is going to dozens of millions but for now the end of the bear cycle is not over and the bottom will likely be end of year

62329158
>>62309515
Buy the fucking dip and stake the little you can, you can never catch the bottom. If you panic sell, it will start pumping.

62329178
I hope this thread can survive another week or two, so we can laugh at OP when Bitcoin dumps to $50k

62329324
>>62329055
There is no bear cycle, it is just retards fire selling so they have free cash to buy into SpaceX IPO and end up inevitably rugged.

The BUYROG will win in the end. I hope this thread stays up until the end od the month, when BTC is at 75k and trending upwards because S&P refused to steal people's retirement to buy the latest AI scam.

62329342
>>62329324
Man, I hope you're right
It's been 8 straight months of dumping, need to see some green for a change

62329345
>>62329324
>52% drop over the last 8 months, with lower and lower bottoms over time
>"There is no bear cycle"

62329476
>>62329342
Serious, dude, just ignore these people (bear posters are just /smg/ bots and /pmg/ twofaced assholes, go tend to your garden, and come back in a few weeks.

The last time it was the same shit. The BUYROG called the bottom, they all laughed and posted bears, then the bottom was kept, and BTC rallied to near 80k before the SpaceX IPO prepping started.

We are frankly better off without these people. BTC is literally outperforming silver and 90% of crypto right now, yet they act like this is the end of crypto (it is not).

The idea of crypto is what matters, and they hate and fear this idea because they will lose, as they have lost many times before. Just zoom out the price charts.

This is not buy x sell, it is good x evil, and we are better off without retards buying into BTC so they can pump and dump.

62329478
>>62309503
/biz/ tricked me into waiting for a lower dip during the last bottom. I'm going to DCA now

62329970
>>62309503
Dunno m8 at my local groxery store they dib't accept bitcoin. Where xan you buy groceries, other than your cherrypicked example?

62331787
>>62329478
/biz/ always tells you to buy the dip. Then the dip comes, and they tell you not to buy it.

62331939
Brought to you by Epstein and Co.: Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value!

62333686
>>62309503
I bought the rally (my first cycle) and the dips/bottom. waiting on the good days now

62333871
>>62309503
30k

62334072
63K REJECTED

62334365
>>62309503
>65K is the bottom
But it went lower anon?

62334381
>>62329970
you can buy anything you want with a coinbase card, what're you on about geez?

62336135
THE BUYROG IS LAUGHING AT ALL OF THE BEAR SHILLS

YOU LOST. YOU SOLD THE BOTTOM

THE BUYROG WINS AGAIN

BEARSBANE

62336205
>>62309503
At least until it dips again. 26K is the real bottom, screen cap this.

62336221
>>62336205
It is never going 55k. I have no idea why PM metal shills (most of the bear is PM fags, we tested it) watch silver get ended by the Fed, and decide to lash out trolling people's financial lives.

I am glad the BUYROG convinced some people to not miss yet another dip.

62336238
>>62336135
>THE BUYROG IS LAUGHING
>still below the 65k "bottom" of OP

62337880
>>62336238
YOU LOST

62340948
Bear bros, our response? The buyrog keeps calling it right.

62341211
>>62340948
Sorry, I can’t hear anything with the noise of all these coins crashing at the same time.

62341507
>>62341211
just bought $20k MSTY

62342143
Your conviction is admirable, but not warranted.

item.title: How hard is EE uni? — 30 replies — 9.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
How hard is EE uni?

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-06-09 19:41 UTC

OP

How hard is EE uni?

Early replies (first 3)

62339490
>>62339486
AI, it's over. Only get licensing for a profession that is protected by the highest levels of corruption.

62339492
very, if you are not comfortable with math and physics, or don't have an iq of at least 120, you will be overwhelmed by the workload and labs and assignments

62339496
>>62339492
Where do the 115-118 IQ engineers go when they realize they're not the next Nikola Tesla?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62339517
>>62339496
maintenance work, going from site to site to diagnose faults, basically a tradie job, or you can join a epc firm and start designing electrical layouts in housing and substations, it really depends on your connections, the really high iq ones are starting their own companies or designing high end chips, i really don't see the point in getting into debt for this degree today with ai about to replace everything, unless you are doing it as a hobby, ai is already doing chip design

62339519
>>62339486
huge field, some are specialties harder than others; you'll make dog shit money for a few years until you get a handle on things and double or triple your salary.
Retards get auto-filtered and forced into finishing a business degree.

62339524
>>62339496
Some got leg lengthening and dental surgery and moved into higher management, but now, there is AI. Understand that.

62339542
>>62339517
Shouldn't EE's have good unions? I'm sure they can fight back against AI for a bit unlike programmers. Ofc they can't stop certain things such as AI making better chips but I'm sure they can retain some job security.

62339546
>>62339496
If they're still at university? Comp-sci. I'm not being a smartass. I was in a EE/comp-sci blended program. Had several classmates go, "I can't deal with the physics prereqs," and jump ship.

62339559
>>62339546
i don't even know why people compare comp sci with ee, comp sci is largely theoretical, most of the practical stuff you learn at work

62339563
>>62339542
ees won't be replaced like programmers, but i don't think new jobs will be created, they will just go to the current crop of engineers, most of the practical maintenance work will still be around, if you really want to hedge your bet, then do nursing or something related, that will likely be the last job that remains in demand

62339565
>>62339546
Huh I thought maybe they'd switch to mechanical engineering

62339569
>>62339486
It was very hard for me. Spent a lot of time having to study for complex math, circuit diagrams, fourier transforms, it was a fucking nightmare. Graduated at the tail end of the great recession, literally no one was hiring. Took a fucking glorified helpdesk job, worked my way up to DevOps because my degree was technically also CompEng, shifted to a different company focusing more on the FinOps side of things, and now I just do DevOps full time. Literally nothing to do with anything I spent 4 years of my life perfecting only to just throw it all away doing cloud work. Had an opportunity to start at micron a decade ago (talk about missed opportunities) but I probably wouldn't have gotten any stock options as a new grad so who knows.
Don't do this unless you want to. I enjoyed it, but what I got out of it more than anything was learning to work with Saudis and Chinks since those were my classmates, but don't even think about it being an instant golden ticket. Life's a journey so don't kick yourself if you have to work from the bottom up.

62339592
>>62339559
I think it's because they attract a lot of the same potential students: "I like computers and electronics." I can only speak for my experience, but the comp sci curriculum involved was mostly practical or foundational stuff: architecture, operating systems, networking, etc. Not the more esoteric math end of it. The mix with EE intended for targeting FPGAs, embedded, robotics, and automation/controls stuff. Basically, "know how to filter a ADC on either side."
Students would take intro to circuits and intro to programming the same year and decide they liked writing Hello World more than calculating Thevenin equivalents.

62339598
>>62339563
Oh boy are you deluded if you think you're some kind of special exception to the rule. You're already obsolete.

Muh Engineering.
Muh special skill.

Bye bye.

62339604
>>62339598
i am not saying they won't be replaced, i am saying it won't happen like programming since its a very wide field

62339621
>>62339592
i didn't know computer scientists do control, although its probably very introductory stuff, and fpgas? How do they even teach you this stuff without understanding circuits at a deeper level, or maybe they only teach how to program them and not how to design an fpga in a pcb,

62339677
Learn analog/digital hardware and some embedded programming, then try to get into industries where they require you to build highly specialized and custom embedded electronics, very lucrative but probably requires touching grass to even begin to know where the opportunities are. In electronics, you can pretty much teach yourself everything just get KiCad and read some Altium blogs to figure out what to do with traces and layer mangement, then send your gerber to some fab in China and you have a finished product. Still need real industry experience and good hardware skills to know what products are actually in demand. Stay away from copying the cool "projects" you see on Youtube, a lot of them are just performative LARPing and the concepts you learn from doing those projects might not be as applicable to what's actually needed in industry.


Never go into utilities or power engineering, the boomers stay there for 50+ years and refuse to to leave, even then they do they'll just hire some 24 year old son of some senior director to replace the boomer that just left.

62339686
>>62339677
Maybe that's still viable, but you need some moat against customers letting AI design & order the product for them. Risk & scale, for example.

62339741
>>62339686
If the AI is good enough build the product from scratch and order the proper components from the correct vendors then it's over. Doesn't matter how hard people cry about it, even if you beg for the government to ban AI to "SAVE MUH JOBS" it won't matter because the Chinese definitely won't give a shit and will go full AI adoption, and in the end the customers will end up ordering from them like they already do with majority of manufactured goods. I wouldn't worry about it until it happens. Just learn something else. If you can do it with EE, then you can do it with something else.

62339767
>>62339741
It can map very complex patterns in IT, and work autonomously for hours. That's because the data is good enough and public, IT cucks lack testosterone. Now AI corporations have access to the IP of every industry. If these API calls can be meaningfully used for training, they will find a way to force the customers to allow it, or just use it and get away with it because it's too profitable. I'd expect 12 to 48 months for end to end EE product development, depending on the political plays.

62339775
>>62339542
EE doesnt need unions. AI is only a threat to shit tier programmers. AI cant into embedded. Its hilarious to watch people try and use AI to make anything that connects to the physical world. It can make slop game, apps, and websites.

62339796
>>62339767
>the data is good enough and public, IT cucks lack testosterone.
the moat would be lucrative opportunities in fields that filter out the IT cucks where you have to service equipment outdoors where there's no ac and where the data is not good enough due to harsh operating conditions because average AI slop product would just get completely torn apart. Instrumentation for downhole drilling for example.

62339951
>>62339796
>harsh, torn apart
So we are back to fighting for a few jobs where the slaves are worked to death? Nice.

62342082
what am I gonna do.

62342136
>>62339542
AI is already used to make better chips. EEs don't give a fuck and just tell each other to git gud.

62342185
>>62339563
I'll just be making a little more than min wage as a nurse. Hours will be long too. I guess it's better than nothing.

62342217
>>62342082
https://ocw.mit.edu/
Teach yourself if you got the brains.
10 months ago I knew nothing. Now I can diagnosis pretty much any circuit AC/DC. I repair guitar amps, music equipment. Design circuits, make them.
look up the syllabus then search textbook_name.pdf. I use AI to make me questions to test myself.
There are lectures on youtube as well.

62342229
>>62342185
registered nurses make over 200k, it's a lucrative job and in extreme high demand

62342598
>>62339486
pretty easy and boring, should be harder desu; my iq is only 105 or so
however it's not optimal if you want to get chicks in college

62342635
>>62339496
industrial engineering or business and dad gets them a job

item.title: Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme. — 18 replies — 5.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme.

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-06-09 19:08 UTC

OP

Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme. Look at this poster who copies and pastes an entire thread just to shit it up and make fun of XRP holders: >>62338460

The funny thing? He forgot to remove the (You) when he copied and pasted the whole thing. But you can see his ID is different.

So my best guess is it's probably a single poster who constantly switches id to force this shitty meme. Probably a chainlink bagholder.

Early replies (first 3)

62339778
Imagine caring this much, holy shit. Adult bully victim no doubt

62339781
>>62339778
Lol of course I don't care so much but it goes to show how pathetic those link tards are trying to force that unfunny boom-xrp meme. The guy thinks if he switches id it looks like more people are in on it but that retard forgot to remove the (You) from his own post.

You are just butthurt you got called out hard and i'm gonna show this to everyone on /biz/ so they know who is really responsible for forcing that dumb meme.

62339829
>>62339778
idk bout that... xrp apparently lives rent free in linkbaggies minds

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62339835
>>62339829
100%
And I caught him red handed

And Chainlinkies be all like: "Durrr imagine caring this isn't important"

62340175
>>62339844
Xrp hasn't reclaimed it's all time high after a whole decade. If xrp baggies didn't sell they have blown their chance of limiting their losses and buying something useful

Kaboom

62340182
>>62339768
Its biepum the yeet node operator
Give him a break, chainlink shilling and xrp fudding is his personality

62340345
>>62339768
so fxcking bullish

62340350
Boooomers and peelers won

62340365
>>62339768
>Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme.
GIGABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

62340374
>>62339768
Why would anyone buy Chainlink or xrp I don't understand. Crypto is there to buy drugs online. Can you buy drugs online with Chainlink ? No. Can you buy drugs online with xrp? No. Case closed.

62340393
>>62339768
I don't hold link either tho
KABOOOOOOOOOOOM

62340516
everybody hates u dumb stinks

62340978
>>62339781
>words words words
It's funny how you're immediately identifiable by the spergy seethe that radiates from your posts.

Even funnier, >>62339844 posting the madmen meme.
>"We don't think about Linkies at all!!"
>Op literally creates a thread ranting about Chainlink because some other random thread that doesn't mention Chainlink could have been made by a Linkie

62340989
>>62340978
>TLDR linkjeet seethe

didnt read lol

62340992
BOOOOOOOOOOM

BULLISH AT XRP

62341232
Bumping this bullsih thread
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62342495
>>62339768
>My retarded shitposts made someone so mad he made an expose thread on me
FXCKING BULLISH KABOOOOOOOOOOOM

i want him released to dab on cryptopoors

62340589

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62340592
HELP ME

62340607
>>62340592
He years to pin the weasel once again

62340667
>>62340556
SBF was a scapegoat who got the book thrown at him because he was the public face of his company, that frumpy bitch who worked with him was just as guilty and got a slap on the wrist.

He deserves a pardon.

62340693
>>62340667
Buy an ad

62340751
He was a good yid that made an honest mistake

62340757
>>62340556

62340774
He did nothing wrong

62340784
I really hope he does get his pardon. Not because I'm basically a genetic relative of his being a mixed-breed half White half ashkenazi shitskin, but because it will open the eyes of boomers to Trump's blatant corruption. Everyone distinctly remembers this pudgy jew was the largest Democratic donor at the time who fiercely (lol) opposed Trump.

Will Trump save his fellow jew?

Captcha: Find the Star of Remphan

62340799
wen sbf gets his pardon /biz/ will be back and better than ever

62340800
>>62340784

62340809
>>62340667
i love her

62340822

62340847
>>62340556
he was a good boy!

>>62340822
he won't be out till he's 57, unless Trump steps in.

62340869
>>62340556

62340902
>>62340809

62340940
